ResearchProjectDef.label: Lighting
ResearchProjectDef.description:
Various lighting fixtures are indispensable tools for human illumination. Ever since the discovery of fire, different types of lighting devices have been continuously invented.

In ancient times, lighting mainly relied on various styles of candlesticks, which burned animal or plant oils to preserve light.

Unlock various candlesticks and oil lamps.

ResearchProjectDef.label: Floor tile
ResearchProjectDef.description: Paving roads with floor tiles facilitated travel for pedestrians, horses, and carts. Beyond practicality, arranging tiles into intricate patterns also became an artistic pursuit.\n\nUnlock various floor tiles and decorative patterns.

ResearchProjectDef.label: Mortise and tenon
ResearchProjectDef.description: Mortise-and-tenon joints are a traditional woodworking technique used by ancient Chinese carpenters. By interlocking protruding and recessed parts of wood, they achieved strong structural connections without the need for nails or glue.\n\nUnlock basic furniture and decorations.

ResearchProjectDef.label: Festival furniture
ResearchProjectDef.description: Lantern Festival, Spring Festival, Dragon Boat Festival—these traditional Chinese holidays are accompanied by abundant related furniture and customs.\n\nUnlock holiday-themed furniture and decorations.

ResearchProjectDef.label: Ming style furniture
ResearchProjectDef.description: Ming-style furniture represents a pinnacle in the history of Chinese furniture. With its graceful, flowing lines, elegant proportions, and simple silhouettes, each piece reflects exquisite craftsmanship.\n\nUnlock Ming-style furniture.

ResearchProjectDef.label: Dynasty furniture
ResearchProjectDef.description: Modern Chinese furniture incorporates elements from various styles, shedding the heaviness and complexity of ancient furniture. It is now more open, lightweight, and primarily made of wood and metal, emphasizing restraint, simplicity, and practicality.\n\nUnlock modern Chinese furniture.

ResearchProjectDef.label: Modern Chinese furniture
ResearchProjectDef.description: Furniture used by nobility in ancient feudal dynasties featured refined materials and luxurious appearances.\n\nUnlock dynasty furniture.
